name: New York
climate: Humid continental -- cold winters, hot humid summers
note: Many NYC apartment buildings have weight or breed restrictions -- always check your lease before adopting.

name: Los Angeles
climate: Mediterranean -- mild, dry, generally warm year-round
note: Warm, dry weather year-round means heatstroke risk is a real concern, especially for flat-faced breeds and cars parked in the sun.

name: Chicago
climate: Humid continental -- very cold winters, warm summers
note: Cold winters mean paw protection (booties or wax) and shorter outdoor time matter for small or short-coated breeds.

name: Houston
climate: Humid subtropical -- hot, humid summers
note: High heat and humidity raise heatstroke risk; morning or evening walks are safer than midday in summer.

name: Phoenix
climate: Desert -- extremely hot, dry summers
note: Pavement temperatures can burn paw pads within seconds in summer -- the 'back of hand on pavement' test is a common local safety check.

name: Philadelphia
climate: Humid continental -- cold winters, hot summers
note: Distinct seasons mean owners typically need both cold-weather and hot-weather gear across the year.

name: San Antonio
climate: Humid subtropical -- hot summers
note: Long hot season makes access to shade and water on walks especially important.

name: San Diego
climate: Mediterranean -- mild year-round
note: Mild year-round weather makes San Diego one of the more walkable, dog-park-friendly climates in the US.

name: Dallas
climate: Humid subtropical -- hot summers
note: Summer heat advisories are common; many local vets recommend limiting exercise during peak afternoon hours.

name: Austin
climate: Humid subtropical -- hot summers
note: Known for a strong pet-friendly culture, with many outdoor restaurants and public spaces welcoming leashed dogs.

name: San Jose
climate: Mediterranean -- mild, dry summers
note: Mild climate year-round supports outdoor exercise for most breeds without major seasonal gear changes.

name: Fort Worth
climate: Humid subtropical -- hot summers
note: Similar heat considerations to nearby Dallas -- shade and hydration on walks are key in summer months.

name: Jacksonville
climate: Humid subtropical -- hot, humid, high flea/tick season
note: Warm, humid climate extends flea and tick season nearly year-round, making parasite prevention a bigger priority.

name: Columbus
climate: Humid continental -- cold winters, warm summers
note: Distinct winters mean cold-weather gear and shorter walks are common considerations for small or thin-coated breeds.

name: Charlotte
climate: Humid subtropical -- hot summers, mild winters
note: Mild winters and hot summers mean flea/tick prevention is typically recommended nearly year-round.

name: San Francisco
climate: Mediterranean -- cool, foggy summers
note: Cooler summer temperatures than much of California make SF comfortable for heat-sensitive breeds most of the year.

name: Indianapolis
climate: Humid continental -- cold winters, warm summers
note: Seasonal extremes mean owners often need both winter coats and summer cooling mats depending on breed.

name: Seattle
climate: Oceanic -- mild, rainy
note: Frequent rain makes waterproof gear and paw-drying routines a common part of daily walks.

name: Denver
climate: Semi-arid -- sunny, low humidity, cold winters
note: High altitude and intense sun mean pets can dehydrate faster on hikes than owners might expect from the temperature alone.

name: Boston
climate: Humid continental -- cold winters, warm summers
note: Cold, icy winters make paw protection and salt/de-icer paw-washing a common local routine.
